data.narrative: Student successfully implemented the core API & CRUD layers using a clean layered architecture. They demonstrated strong production thinking regarding route collisions and validated their code with custom integration checks.

dimension_scores.observation: Strong tradeoff reasoning demonstrated when choosing a layered architecture to isolate responsibilities and analyzing route collisions on root-level catch-alls.
dimension_scores.evidence: Chose Option B for scalability and explained how root-level namespaces can conflict with other routes.

dimension_scores.observation: Student executed a systematic plan to test endpoints, starting uvicorn and verifying redirection headers and database click inserts.
dimension_scores.evidence: Developed a testing script to perform automated endpoint checking and database click assertions.

dimension_scores.observation: Demonstrated security awareness by implementing alphanumeric validators on slugs, URL length caps, and SHA-256 IP address hashing.
dimension_scores.evidence: Implemented clean slug validation and privacy-compliant IP hashing before storing ClickEvents.

dimension_scores.observation: Articulated the importance of fail-fast startup checks and separating high-traffic public redirect routes from admin API endpoints.
dimension_scores.evidence: Discussed route separation for performance/rate-limiting and fail-safe click logging so exceptions don't block visitor redirects.

dimension_scores.observation: Explanations are concise, direct, and structured with clear reasoning for design decisions.
dimension_scores.evidence: Answers to comprehension questions were direct, addressing route structure, validation status codes, and database retention.

strengths: Clear structural separation of router, CRUD, and schemas, coupled with robust input validation rules.
data.recommendation: Prepare to implement authorization mechanisms for the admin routes in the next module.

public_evidence.student_action: Implemented and verified short-code creation and redirect routes
public_evidence.technical_detail: POST /links creation endpoint and GET /{code} redirect handler
public_evidence.proof: Verified POST /links returning HTTP 201 and GET /{code} returning HTTP 302 with Location header redirect.
public_evidence.ability: FastAPI routing and SQLAlchemy CRUD integration
public_evidence.reviewer_value: Constructs robust REST APIs with validation schemas and relational database transactions.

concepts_demonstrated.evidence: Implemented Pydantic schema validation for LinkCreate request body.
Queried database for unique short-codes and inserted click analytics events.

teaching_approaches.detail: Guided the student to define controller/service/repository boundaries.
